home.description: Eight different website types, so you can see at a glance what fits your business best.
home.note: Each demo is built as if it were a real project, not a generic template. It's the best way to picture what your own site could look like.

closing.title: Can't find exactly what you're after?
closing.text: No two businesses are the same, so these eight demos are just a starting point. Tell me what you need and we'll build it around you.

demo.placeholder: Here is where your project, your website, your demo will be shown — whatever you need to present.

hero.title: Espacio Verde Foundation
hero.subtitle: For twelve years we've been bringing environmental information to anyone willing to listen: neighbours, schools, local councils and anyone curious about the world around them.

about.title: Who we are
about.text1: It all started in 2013 with a group of neighbours from Quart de Poblet worried about the state of the local orchards and the nearby river. What began as informal meetings in a borrowed room eventually became a foundation dedicated to gathering and sharing environmental information carefully and without rushing.
about.text2: Today we still hold the same idea: we don't sell anything, and we don't ask for donations on every page. We simply make available the data, reports and resources we put together year after year.

areas.title: Information areas
areas.intro: We organise our work around four main areas, each with its own documentation and regular updates.

biodiversidad.title: Biodiversity
biodiversidad.description: Species records of local flora and fauna, annual surveys and monitoring of the ecosystems closest to the Valencian orchards.

climatico.title: Climate change
climatico.description: Temperature, rainfall and drought data for the region, explained so that anyone can follow them, without unnecessary jargon.

educacion.title: Environmental education
educacion.description: Classroom-ready materials: worksheets, games and guides regularly used by the schools we work with.

voluntariado.title: Volunteering
voluntariado.description: Everything you need to know to help out from time to time: tree planting, riverbank clean-ups and data collection days.

recursos.title: Available resources
recursos.intro: Part of our work is writing down what we learn, so it isn't lost and anyone can look it up whenever they need to.

guias.title: Educational guides
guias.description: Short, plain-language documents on specific topics: how to identify an injured bird, what to do during a heatwave in the orchard, and more.

informes.title: Annual reports
informes.description: A summary of the data collected throughout the year, with simple charts and comparisons against previous years.

charlas.title: Talks and events
charlas.description: A calendar of public events, usually held at schools or the local library, on current environmental topics.

q1.question: Can I use your reports for a school project?
q1.answer: Of course. All the information we publish is freely available, we just ask that you credit the source when you use it.

q2.question: Do I need to be a member to receive the guides?
q2.answer: No, there's no membership or fee of any kind. Anyone can write to us and we'll send the material they need.

q3.question: How often do you update the data?
q3.answer: Annual reports are published every January, although some species records are revised as soon as we detect relevant changes.

contact.title: Contact information
contact.intro: If you have any questions about our resources, or simply want to know more, feel free to write to us.
contact.schedule: Monday to Friday, 9:00 am to 2:00 pm

hero.title: Naranja Studio
hero.subtitle: Handmade ceramics, one piece at a time, from a small workshop where every vase or cup takes its time to be ready. Nothing here is mass-produced.

productos.title: Fresh from the workshop
productos.intro: Every piece is fired and glazed by hand, so no two are ever quite the same — and that's the whole point.

artesanal.title: Handmade

resenas.title: What people who already own a piece say

r1.text: I bought the water jug thinking it'd just be a treat, and now it's the first thing I reach for every morning.
r1.author: Marina, Valencia

r2.text: You can tell each mug is different from the next. It arrived with a handwritten note, such a lovely touch.
r2.author: Adrián, Castellón

r3.text: I ordered the plate set as a gift and ended up buying another one for myself. The finish is incredible.
r3.author: Laura, Alicante

hero.title: El Cajón de Recetas
hero.subtitle: Old family recipes, a bit of garden talk, and small stories about life in Valencia, told slowly, the way things are told in a kitchen.

blog.searchPlaceholder: Search a recipe, tradition or topic
blog.noResults: We couldn't find any article matching those filters. Try a different word.

arrozHorno.title: My grandmother's baked rice, exactly as she taught me
arrozHorno.excerpt: With yesterday's chickpeas, black pudding, and that crust that forms on top if you keep an eye on the oven. The old recipe, no shortcuts.

mascleta.title: Why the mascleta is felt in your chest before it's heard
mascleta.excerpt: It's not just noise: there's a structure, a planned crescendo, and a tradition behind those two minutes that stop the city every March afternoon.

plantarJulio.title: What to plant in July without the heat ruining everything
plantarJulio.excerpt: July shows no mercy in the garden, but some varieties hold up well if you know when to water and where to find some shade.

mercadoColon.title: A slow walk through the Mercado de Colón
mercadoColon.excerpt: It's neither the cheapest nor the biggest, but there's something about it that makes you linger longer than planned. Here's where to start.

horchata.title: Homemade horchata, step by step, without it splitting
horchata.excerpt: Good tiger nuts make all the difference, but the real trick is in the resting time. Here's the recipe just as my aunt made it every June.

origenPaella.title: Where paella really comes from (and why it's so debated)
origenPaella.excerpt: Between the Albufera, field labourers, and centuries of variations, the story of paella is worth far more than a lunch-table argument.

riegoCalor.title: How to water the garden when the thermometer won't drop below 38
riegoCalor.excerpt: It's not about watering more, it's about watering smarter. A few simple tricks we've used in the family garden for years.

sidebar.aboutText: Written from Valencia by Marisa and Toni, between family recipes and time spent in the garden. No filters, no rush.
sidebar.newsletterText: Once a week, nothing more, whenever there's something truly worth sharing.
